published.text:
### Hero
Heading: Missed Call Text-Back vs. Voicemail for Painting Contractors
Body: If your painting company misses calls, missed-call text-back is usually a better fallback than voicemail when the real problem is simple quote-call recovery. Voicemail asks the homeowner to leave details, wait for a callback, and trust that someone will respond before they call the next painter. Missed-call text-back answers the miss immediately and gives the homeowner a clear next step while the quote request is still active.
Sub: Below: when missed-call text-back is the smarter first move for a painting contractor, when voicemail is still acceptable, when the phone problem has already grown past both and needs heavier live AI phone answering, and what the existing painting plus phone-recovery proof honestly supports.

### Core sections
- Extracts the exact lighter-fallback buyer decision already implied by the live painting missed-call page and its tracked prompts: stay with voicemail or move to SMS-first recovery.
- Keeps the frame tightly on painting realities: homeowner quote-shopping, owners and crews being unavailable during the workday, callback ownership, and the point where voicemail is too slow to protect an estimate opportunity.
- Preserves clear separation from `/ai-automation-for-painting-contractors`, `/what-to-automate-first-for-painting-contractors`, and `/missed-call-text-back-for-painting-contractors` by isolating the narrower voicemail-vs-text-back choice.
- Uses the live painting cluster, the Paris Cafe call-handling case study, and already-live roofing / landscaping sibling voicemail-comparison pages as adjacent proof without pretending there is already a direct painting-only comparison case study.

### FAQ
Subhead: Straight answers for painting-company owners deciding whether voicemail is still good enough after missed calls
Topics: whether text-back is usually better than voicemail, when voicemail is still acceptable, when a painter should skip text-back and move to live answering, whether homeowners reply to missed-call texts, and how this page differs from the other painting phone pages.

### Final CTA
Heading: Need a practical answer on missed-call recovery for your painting company?
Body: Book a 30-minute call. We will look at your missed-call pattern, whether voicemail is quietly costing estimate opportunities, and whether your company should use missed-call text-back, move to live AI phone answering, or leave the phone stack alone for now.

edits.description: Shipped because the roadmap backlog is still exhausted but the live painting phone cluster still contained one exact unisolated comparison query already present in tracked prompts.

The page stays meaningfully distinct by focusing only on the lighter fallback buyer decision instead of rewording the broader painting parent, the first-project page, or the live missed-call workflow page.
Tone stays practical and non-hype with explicit fit / not-fit framing, homeowner quote-shopping context, and clear boundaries around what text-back and voicemail each solve.
Proof framing stays disciplined: live painting pages, direct call-handling proof from Paris Cafe, and already-live home-service sibling comparison pages.
